_meta.hint: nightly LC generation; item 3 situation swapped from the assigned 'cheese dinner party' (already in DO-NOT-REUSE list) to a distinct pizza/party-planning situation to avoid duplication

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I need new music. I've been listening to the same three pop singers for months and I'm so bored.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Have you tried anything outside pop? I've been really into this indie folk band called Hollow Pines lately.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Indie folk? Really?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, it's mellow, mostly acoustic guitar and vocal harmonies. Their album 'Riverbend' is a good place to start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds really different from what I usually play. Can you send me the link tonight?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, I'll text it tonight. Skip the first track though, it's just an instrumental intro.

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman try?

options.A: An indie folk band called Hollow Pines
options.B: A pop singer she has never heard before
options.C: A jazz playlist he made himself
options.D: The woman's favorite pop album again

questions.explanation: 男生说他最近在听一个叫Hollow Pines的独立民谣乐队，并推荐她尝试，所以A正确。B把独立民谣错误地说成流行歌手，属于wrong_detail；C爵士歌单对话中未提及，属于related_but_not_stated；D把男生的推荐错误地归为女生自己喜欢的流行专辑，属于misattributed。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Attend a live concert by the band next week
options.B: Ask the man to teach her guitar
options.C: Listen to the album once it's sent
options.D: Keep listening only to her usual pop singers

questions.explanation: 女生请男生把链接发给她，男生答应今晚发送，所以她接下来最可能是收到链接后听这张专辑，C正确。A去看乐队现场演出属于过度推断，属于over_inference；B请男生教吉他与对话内容无关，属于unrelated_next_step；D女生本来就是因为厌倦了原来的流行歌手才想找新音乐，继续只听原来的歌手与她的意图相反，属于rejected_option。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I saw that new mystery thriller last night. Have you heard about it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, really? How was it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The plot twists were incredible, I didn't see the ending coming. But it's almost three hours long.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours is a lot. Is it worth sitting through that whole time?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Mostly, yeah, I just got a little restless near the end.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll probably wait for it to stream instead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good idea. Just don't read any reviews, the twists are worth the surprise.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: A concert they are planning to attend together
options.B: A movie the woman saw, twists and length
options.C: The exact ticket price for the mystery thriller
options.D: The man's growing disappointment with the ending of the film

questions.explanation: 两人主要在讨论女生刚看的一部悬疑片，包括它的情节反转和片长问题，所以B正确。A音乐会话题在对话中完全没有出现，属于wrong_topic；C只是背景细节，票价并非讨论重点，属于too_narrow；D把情况完全说反了——男生根本还没看这部电影，也没有表示失望，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Go to the theater to see it this weekend
options.B: Convince the woman to watch it again with him
options.C: Write a detailed review of the plot twists
options.D: Wait for the movie to stream instead

questions.explanation: 男生说自己大概会等这部电影上线流媒体再看，所以D正确。A去电影院看是男生已经放弃的选项，属于rejected_option；B说服女生再看一遍对话中未提及，属于unrelated_next_step；C写详细影评远超对话内容所支持的范围，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two students deciding how many pizzas and drinks to order for a birthday party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How many pizzas do you think we should order for Maya's birthday party this Saturday afternoon?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We're expecting around fifteen people, so four large pizzas should be enough.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Four might be too few if a lot of people show up. Let's get five, just in case.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good point. What about drinks?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Let's do a mix of soda and sparkling water, since not everyone likes soda, and maybe some lemonade too.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'll place the order online tonight so it arrives on time.

questions.stem: What will the man and woman serve besides pizza?

options.A: Only soda, since it's easier to serve
options.B: Coffee and tea for the guests
options.C: A mix of soda and sparkling water
options.D: Just sparkling water only, since Maya seems to prefer it

questions.explanation: 女生提议苏打水和气泡水都准备一些，因为不是所有人都喜欢苏打水，所以C正确。A只准备苏打水与对话内容相反（他们决定混合两种），属于wrong_detail；B咖啡和茶对话中未提及，属于related_but_not_stated；D把想法错误地归因给Maya的个人喜好，对话中并未提到这点，属于misattributed。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Place the order online later tonight
options.B: Drive to the store to buy more pizzas
options.C: Change the party's guest list to fewer people
options.D: Print out a party invitation for guests

questions.explanation: 男生说他今晚会在网上下单，所以A正确。B开车去买更多披萨对话中未提及，属于unrelated_next_step；C减少客人数量远超对话所暗示的内容，属于over_inference；D打印派对邀请函与对话内容无关，属于related_but_not_stated。

items.situation: two friends deciding whether to buy concert tickets now or wait

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you seen ticket prices for the fall concert yet? They're still pretty high.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I checked yesterday. I was thinking we should wait a couple of weeks to see whether the prices actually drop.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's risky, though.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Prices usually drop closer to the date if it's not selling well.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I don't know. I'd rather pay a little more now and know for sure that we have seats.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good, let's just buy them tonight then, so we don't have to keep worrying about it.

questions.stem: What are the man and woman mainly discussing?

options.A: Complaints about the sound quality at recent past concerts
options.B: The exact date when the concert takes place
options.C: A plan to sell their extra concert tickets
options.D: Whether to buy concert tickets now or later

questions.explanation: 两人在讨论是现在就买音乐会门票还是再等等看价格会不会下降，所以D正确。A音响质量的抱怨在对话中完全没有提到，属于wrong_topic；B具体日期只是背景信息，并非讨论重点，属于too_narrow；C卖掉多余门票与对话内容相反——他们是在讨论要不要买，而不是卖，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What will the man and woman most likely do?

options.A: Wait several more weeks to buy tickets
options.B: Purchase the tickets tonight, not later
options.C: Ask the venue for a group discount
options.D: Try to resell their tickets for profit

questions.explanation: 男生最后说“Good, let's just buy them tonight”，所以他们决定今晚就买票，B正确。A继续等待是他们最终放弃的选项，属于rejected_option；C向场馆申请团体折扣对话中未提及，属于unrelated_next_step；D转卖门票获利远超对话内容所支持的范围，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two friends sharing cooking tips while grocery shopping

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Could you help me pick out stir-fry ingredients? I've never cooked one before.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure. The key is prepping everything before you turn on the heat, since it cooks fast.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. I was thinking chicken, broccoli, and some kind of sauce.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That works. Grab pre-cut vegetables too, it'll save a lot of chopping time.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good idea. Should I just buy a bottled sauce, or make my own?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A quick mix of soy sauce, garlic, and cornstarch tastes better and takes two minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Alright, I'll get those. Thanks!

questions.stem: What are the man and woman mainly doing?

options.A: Discussing how to prepare a stir-fry while shopping
options.B: Comparing grocery prices at two different stores
options.C: Deciding which brand of bottled sauce to buy
options.D: Debating whether to cook at home or eat out

questions.explanation: 两人一边在超市购物，一边讨论如何准备炒菜，包括食材和酱料的做法，所以A正确。B比较不同商店的价格在对话中完全没有出现，属于wrong_topic；C只涉及酱料这一个细节，并非整体讨论内容，属于too_narrow；D在家做饭还是外食的争论与对话内容相反——他们已经决定要自己做炒菜了，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely buy for the sauce?

options.A: Buy a bottled sauce from the store shelf
options.B: Return the pre-cut vegetables to the shelf
options.C: Buy the ingredients for a homemade stir-fry sauce
options.D: Sign up for a cooking class at the store

questions.explanation: 男生建议用酱油、大蒜和玉米淀粉自己调酱汁，味道更好且只需两分钟，女生最后说会去买这些食材，所以C正确。A直接买瓶装酱料是女生本来考虑但被男生的建议取代的选项，属于rejected_option；B把切好的蔬菜放回去对话中未提及，属于unrelated_next_step；D报名烹饪课远超对话内容所支持的范围，属于over_inference。
